wine_museums.name: Cartoixa d'Escaladei
wine_museums.address: Camino de la Cartoixa s/n, 43379 Escaladei, Tarragona, Spain
wine_museums.admission: Adults EUR 5; reduced EUR 3.50; guided tours available
wine_museums.description: The 12th-century Carthusian monastery where Priorat viticulture originated in 1163; the preserved ruins and chapter house tell the story of the appellation's viticultural origins.

verified.address_quoted: Camino de la Cartoixa s/n, Escaladei

wine_museums.name: Cooperativa Falset Marca Heritage Museum
wine_museums.address: Avinguda de la Catalunya 2, 43730 Falset, Tarragona, Spain
wine_museums.admission: Included in cooperative tour fee; adults from EUR 5
wine_museums.description: The 1919 Cesar Martinell modernist cooperative building; interpretive panels trace cooperative winemaking history in the Priorat zone from phylloxera recovery to the present.

wine_museums.name: Centre de Visitants del Priorat
wine_museums.admission: Free entry; tasting sessions priced separately
wine_museums.description: The DOQ Priorat denomination's official visitor centre in Falset; permanent exhibition on llicorella geology, varietal identification and the 1989 renaissance, plus tasting sessions.

wine_museums.name: Museu de les Mines de Bellmunt del Priorat
wine_museums.address: Carrer de la Mina s/n, 43738 Bellmunt del Priorat, Tarragona, Spain
wine_museums.admission: Adults EUR 5; guided underground tour of lead-mine tunnels and mineral exhibits
wine_museums.description: Underground lead-mine museum in Bellmunt explaining how the same llicorella slate that defines Priorat wine was once mined for galena; connects the wine and geological heritage of the zone.

verified.address_quoted: Carrer de la Mina s/n, Bellmunt del Priorat

wine_museums.name: Museu Comarcal del Priorat
wine_museums.admission: Adults EUR 3; reduced EUR 1.50; free first Sunday of the month
wine_museums.description: The Priorat comarca museum in Falset covering archaeology, ethnology and rural life including viticulture tools and cooperage artefacts from centuries of winemaking in the region.
